channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Details from New Mexico's lawsuit against Snap show site failed to act on reports of sextortion
item.description: Snapchat failed to act on “rampant” reports of child grooming, sextortion and other dangers to minors on its platform, according to a newly unredacted complaint against the company filed by New Mexico's attorney general. Attorney General Raúl Torrez filed the original complaint on Sept.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: iPhone stuck in SOS mode? Here's how to get out of it
item.description: There are ongoing reports of a widespread outage impacting multiple cellphone and internet providers Monday morning. According to Downdetector.com, AT&T, Verizon and T-Mobile users have all experienced issues.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, NewsNation

item.title: New leaks suggest AMD’s RDNA 4-based GPUs will launch early 2025
item.description: AMD’s next-gen lineup of RDNA 4-based graphics cards are now expected to launch in 2025, according to sources close to YouTube channel Moore’s Law Is Dead.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: This state is suing YouTube for fueling a mental health crisis
item.description: Arkansas sued YouTube and parent company Alphabet on Monday, saying the video-sharing platform is made deliberately addictive and fueling a mental health crisis among youth in the state.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: AppliedXL and Associated Press team up to use AI to sift through federal regulations
item.description: In an era when local newsrooms are stretched thinner than ever, keeping up with the constant deluge of government regulations and updates can feel impossible.
item.dc:creator: Janya Sundar, Fast Company

item.title: US port strike has CIOs prepping for supply chain implications
item.description: Dockworkers have launched a widespread strike at ports across the eastern US from Texas to Maine, with wages and automation topping their list of labor concerns. The stoppage, the first in decades, could cause price hikes and supply shortages, depending on how long it lasts, the AP reports.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Motorola Expands Business Phone Portfolio with Two New Devices
item.description: Motorola launches ThinkPhone25 and moto g75 5G business edition, catering to various business types with enhanced security software
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: The 2024 election is razor close. TikTok creators could tip it
item.description: If Barack Obama’s digital journey to the White House in 2008 marked the Facebook election, and Donald Trump’s social media presence in 2016 made that race the Twitter election, then 2024 seems likely to be remembered as the TikTok election.
item.dc:creator: Joe Berkowitz, Fast Company

item.title: Judge partially dismisses FTC's antitrust lawsuit against Amazon
item.description: A federal judge on Monday partially dismissed the Federal Trade Commission’s (FTC) antitrust lawsuit against Amazon. However, few details are currently available about U.S. District Judge John Chun’s decision, which was filed under seal.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: The Resilience Imperative: Navigating Uncertainty in Business with Digital-First Customer Service
item.description: In a world plagued by uncertainty, businesses must adapt to ensure resiliency. LivePerson's Ruth Zive explores the power of digital-first customer service and AI in navigating disruptions and keeping customers at the forefront.
item.dc:creator: Ruth Zive, Fast Company

item.title: California’s effort to find appropriate AI rules is a fool’s mission
item.description: When California Gov. Gavin Newsom vetoed a key piece of AI oversight legislation Sunday, he said he did so because the measure “falls short of providing a flexible, comprehensive solution to curbing the potential catastrophic risks.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Cruise fined $1.5M over robotaxi crash reporting
item.description: General Motors's autonomous vehicle unit, Cruise, will pay a $1.5 million fine after it failed to disclose details initially about a serious crash involving a pedestrian last year, the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA) said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: FAA Grounds SpaceX's Falcon 9 Rocket: Investigation Launched
item.description: The Federal Aviation Administration has grounded SpaceX's Falcon 9 rocket pending an investigation into the second stage missing its deorbit landing target zone during the Crew-9 launch from Cape Canaveral.
item.dc:creator: Richard Tribou, The Mercury News

item.title: JRuby 10 due to arrive in early-2025
item.description: JRuby, which dates back to 2001 as a Ruby language implementation for the JVM, is set to arrive at version 10 shortly after the new year. JRuby 10 promises to be fully compatible with Ruby 3.4.0, a planned update to Ruby that brings changes for frozen string literals and class updates.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: What to stream: Coldplay, an unhinged Charlie Puth, 'Salem’s Lot,' Sarah Paulson and NHL 25
item.description: Coldplay's 10th studio album and the American Music Awards celebrating their 50th anniversary are some of the new television, films, music and games headed to a device near you.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Trump says he's spoken to Musk about getting Starlink for areas damaged by Helene
item.description: Former President Trump on Monday said he’s spoken with Elon Musk about providing Starlink internet service for those without power in states ravaged by Hurricane Helene. Trump visited Valdosta, Ga., where he toured storm damage and expressed support for those dealing with the aftermath.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Exxon Mobil says advanced recycling is the answer to plastic waste. But is it really?
item.description: Despite its seemingly eco-friendly name, the attorney general’s lawsuit denounced advanced recycling as a “public relations stunt” that largely involves superheating plastics to convert them into fuel.
item.dc:creator: Tribune News Service, The Mercury News

item.title: Elon Musk: Voting for Trump 'only way' to 'save' democracy
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk said Sunday that former President Trump must win the election in November if America wants to preserve its democracy. “Very few Americans realize that, if Trump is NOT elected, this will be the last election,” Musk posted on the social platform X.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: 6 ways to apply automation in devsecops
item.description: The goal of devsecops is to integrate security throughout the software development life cycle. To do this, the security team can’t focus on just the most obvious software development vulnerabilities or just the processes that are the easiest to secure.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: California governor vetoes bill to create first-in-nation AI safety measures
item.description: The decision is a blow to efforts attempting to rein in the industry that is rapidly evolving with little oversight.
item.dc:creator: TRÂN NGUYỄN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Musk faces $1.9 million fine to end X ban in Brazil
item.description: Brazil's Supreme Court said that the social platform X, owned by Elon Musk, owes a fine of 10 million reais, which is about $1.9 million, to end its ban in the country, building off a separate $3.3 million fine previously ordered by the court, Reuters reported on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, The Hill

item.title: Beware of the new sneaky parking QR code scam
item.description: While you may feel pretty sharp at spotting online fraud, there's a sneaky new scam involving QR codes and parking meters across the country affecting even the most cautious people.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Microsoft adds safety tools to Azure AI Studio
item.description: Now that the initial euphoria about generative AI’s capabilities has worn off, reality is setting in: Large language models without appropriate safeguards can be remarkably naïve and are all too willing to share all they know.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Cloudflare accelerates its network with security, traffic optimizations
item.description: Cloudflare unwrapped a series of networking and security innovations during its birthday week event. The announcements include implementations of new and emerging internet standards that could have a wide-ranging impact over time.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Amazon's $4 billion partnership with AI startup Anthropic gets UK competition clearance
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Britain's competition watchdog said Friday that it's clearing Amazon's partnership with artificial intelligence company Anthropic because the $4 billion deal didn't qualify for further scrutiny.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: X requests it be reinstated in Brazil after complying with judge's orders, sources say
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — In the high-stakes showdown between the world’s richest man and a Brazilian Supreme Court justice, Elon Musk blinked. Musk’s social media site X has complied with Alexandre de Moraes’ orders and requested its service be reestablished in the country, two sources said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: GABRIELA SÁ PESSOA and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: OpenAI Chief Technology Officer Mira Murati and 2 other execs are leaving the ChatGPT maker
item.description: A high-ranking executive at OpenAI who served a few days as its interim CEO during a period of turmoil last year said she's leaving the artificial intelligence company.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News
